meta.status: POPULATED — verified programme references; named examples require cultivation

investorCategories.name: Heritage-aligned philanthropic trusts
investorCategories.description: UK trusts with explicit heritage buildings, conservation, or arts mandates; several have funded NI projects.
investorCategories.engagementModel: Capital grant or restricted donation; typically requires CCNI-registered charity and match plan.

examples.name: The Pilgrim Trust
examples.previousGiving: UK heritage capital and organisational development; NI projects via national programmes
examples.fernhillUse: Feasibility match; capital match layer in Phase 2

examples.name: Wolfson Foundation
examples.fernhillUse: Phase B capital match once designs and cost plan exist

examples.name: Garfield Weston Foundation
examples.previousGiving: Supports AHF NI Harnessing Heritage programme nationally
examples.fernhillUse: Development or capital match; aligns with AHF ecosystem

examples.name: Esmée Fairbairn Foundation
examples.fernhillUse: Larger capital or blended finance once governance mature

investorCategories.name: Northern Ireland place-based funders
investorCategories.description: Funders with NI delivery focus and community/heritage alignment.
investorCategories.engagementModel: Grant; often via themed funds or donor-advised structures.

examples.name: Community Foundation for Northern Ireland (CFNI)
examples.knownInterests: Community development, young people, older people, place-based funds
examples.previousGiving: Administers multiple funds including corporate and dormant-accounts-linked programmes

examples.name: National Lottery Community Fund — Dormant Assets for All (NI)
examples.fernhillUse: Trustee training, governance setup, engagement systems

examples.name: The American Ireland Fund
examples.knownInterests: Peace, reconciliation, culture, heritage on island of Ireland

investorCategories.name: Social investment and patient capital
investorCategories.description: Repayable finance for charities and social enterprises; bridges grant drawdown gaps.
investorCategories.engagementModel: Loan or blended finance; requires business plan and security/asset narrative.

examples.name: Charity Bank
examples.knownInterests: Charities and social enterprises with asset-backed or strong cashflow cases
examples.previousGiving: UK charity property and working capital loans
examples.fernhillUse: Bridge between grant award and construction cashflow (Year 0–1 of build)

examples.name: Ulster Community Investment Trust (UCIT)
examples.knownInterests: NI social enterprises and community businesses
examples.previousGiving: NI community asset and enterprise finance
examples.fernhillUse: Fit-out or working-capital bridge if trading subsidiary cashflow supports

examples.name: Social Investment Scotland (SIS)
examples.fernhillUse: Secondary option if UCIT/Charity Bank terms less favourable

investorCategories.name: Corporate sponsorship and CSR
investorCategories.description: Belfast and NI firms with active CSR, skills volunteering, and sponsorship budgets.
investorCategories.engagementModel: Sponsorship, in-kind professional services, named gifts, staff volunteering.

examples.name: Openreach (via CFNI Openreach Community Fund)
examples.knownInterests: Grassroots groups; young people, older people, disability inclusion
examples.typicalGrants: Small grants via CFNI (income thresholds apply)
examples.fernhillUse: Early community programming or accessibility improvements

examples.name: Belfast law, construction, and professional services firms (cultivation list)
examples.previousGiving: Varies — require desk research per firm CSR report
examples.fernhillUse: Launch event sponsorship, skills pro bono (legal, QS, marketing)
cultivationTargets: Tughan & Co / local construction firms (heritage fit-out supply chain)
Belfast solicitors with charity practices (governance pro bono)
Regional banks (CSR and branch community funds)

investorCategories.name: Community shares and crowdfunding
investorCategories.description: Non-repayable or withdrawable community investment alongside charity structure.
investorCategories.engagementModel: Community shares (FCA-regulated offer) or rewards crowdfunding.

examples.name: Community Benefit Society share offer
examples.previousGiving: UK heritage and pub/community asset precedents
examples.fernhillUse: Match funding layer; sits alongside CLG — legal advice required

examples.name: Spacehive / Crowdfunder UK
examples.fernhillUse: Public phase of match funding after planning route clear

tier1.approach: Chair/trustee cultivation; named capital gift; multi-year pledge

tier2.approach: Named room/garden opportunity; trust application with full pack

tier3.approach: Community giving, crowdfunding, local business sponsorship
